intro: Barosma betulina, also known as the round-peeled pepper tree, is a plant native to South Africa. Its resin or gum is used in traditional medicine and has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The gum is also used as a flavoring agent in the production of certain soft drinks, due to its unique and pungent flavor profile.
tea: Barosma betulina gum tea, also known as Buchu tea, is a traditional herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including urinary tract infections and digestive problems. The tea is made from the roots and leaves of the Barosma betulina plant, which is native to South Africa. Drinking buchu tea is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular natural remedy for many health conditions.

blend: Barosma betulina gum blend is a type of herbal mixture that typically combines the aromatic properties of Barosma betulina, also known as the "pee tree," with other ingredients. blend recipe: Barosma betulina gum, when used in traditional aromatherapy and perfumery, is often blended with other ingredients such as bergamot essential oil, ylang-ylang, and sandalwood. recipe: To make barosma betulina gum for medicinal use, the roots of the Barosma betulina plant are typically harvested and dried to prepare them for the gum-making process. The dried roots are then boiled in water, creating a decoction, which is strained and concentrated to create a syrup-like consistency. This syrup is then mixed with gum arabic or other natural adhesive substances to create a gum that can be used for medicinal purposes.
